title: coleus psammophilus
intro: Coleus psammophilus, a plant species native to India, is a type of flowering plant commonly known as the 'Sand Coleus' or 'Dusty Coleus'. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as wounds and skin infections. The health benefits of Coleus psammophilus include its ability to aid in the reduction of swelling and pain, as well as its potential to exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Coleus psammophilus plant are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
